subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Follows a clear logical progression from introduction through data to conclusion
Well-defined structure with a Table of Contents, section headings and numbered subsections
Conclusion neatly ties results back to the research question
weaknesses: Lengthy textbook-style definitions add unnecessary bulk
Table of Contents omits page numbers and contains blank rows
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (coherence & organization) meet the band-3 descriptors for clear logical development and good structure; the conciseness strand is band 2 due to padding and repetition. Holistically, this aligns with a score of 3 out of 4.

strengths: Uses standard statistical notation (r, Î£, ÏÂ²) and correctly rounds the Pearson result
Includes a labelled scatterplot and a comprehensive numerical table
Observed-frequencies table is clearly presented and supports the ÏÂ² calculation
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (typos in Î£, missing subscripts) and mis-typed Qâ values
Limited to two representations; lacks box-plots or residual plots
Switching between prose and symbols causes occasional clarity lapses
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands are at band 2 (âsome appropriate mathematical language and notationâ, âsome representationsâ, âmostly clear with inconsistenciesâ). This warrants 2 out of 4.

strengths: Includes a personal anecdote about living in the âsunshine stateâ
Selects topic and data sources independently
weaknesses: Analysis follows routine statistical recipes with little novel insight
Personal approach does not drive the mathematical strategy
Creativity is limited to a basic outlier decision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand shows only limited evidence of independent thinking, personal approach and creativity, fitting band 1. Hence 1 out of 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on lack of expected correlation and study limitations
Basic critique of data quality (year mismatch, confounding variables)
Clear link between results and personal understanding of melanoma risk
weaknesses: Reflection remains descriptive; lacks deeper statistical critique (effect sizes, confidence intervals)
Brief mention of Yates correction needs further explanation
Uniform residuals in ÏÂ² are not analysed in depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands achieve band 2 for meaningful reflection, basic critical analysis and clear connection to understanding, justifying 2 out of 3.

strengths: Correct application of Pearsonâs r and ÏÂ² methods within the SL syllabus
Systematic presentation of x, y, xÂ², yÂ² computations
Expected frequencies and degrees of freedom are calculated accurately
weaknesses: Copying errors in formulas (â44 vs âx), mis-stated correlation range
Inconsistent Qâ values and outlier-rule bound misapplication
Several computational inaccuracies undermine full correctness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the relevance and conceptual understanding reach band 3, accuracy is at band 2 (âpartially correct mathematicsâ). Under IBâs holistic criterion, the accuracy shortfall limits the overall criterion to 2 out of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student selects the topic and data sources independently, but the mathematical techniques (scatterplot, Pearson r, chi-square) are standard recipes with little adaptation.  There is minimal commentary that shows novel insight beyond textbook procedure, giving only âlimited evidence of independent thinking.â

Explanations of Pearson r and chi-square demonstrate a generally good understanding, and the student correctly interprets statistical results (weak negative correlation, fail to reject Hâ).  Mis-statements are occasional rather than pervasive, aligning with âgood understanding in parts.â

Several computational inaccuracies appear: the r numerator shows a copying error, sums in the formula are mis-typed, and Qâ is inconsistently recorded.  These errors raise doubts about full correctness, so the work is best described as âpartially correct.â

Much of the content is relevant, but lengthy textbook-style definitions (scatterplots, outlier rule, chi-square explanation) and repeated references to external sources add unnecessary bulk.  At ~2770 words the text feels padded; tighter phrasing would improve conciseness.  Hence it aligns with âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements.â

Standard statistical notation is used (r, Î£, ÏÂ²), and formulas are written mathematically.  However, there are inconsistencies: the numerator of r is mis-copied ("â 44"), IQR is typed once as 1.9 then used incorrectly, and variables in the chi-square table lack proper subscripts.  Such issues prevent a âmostly appropriate throughoutâ judgement, placing the strand in band 2.

Explanations are generally understandable, yet small inconsistencies (e.g., mis-stating Qâ as 5.7 after earlier giving Qâ = 5.8, mis-typed Î£x) reduce overall clarity.  Units are sometimes omitted and switching between words and symbols is uneven.  The strand therefore sits at âmostly clear with some consistency.â

Pearsonâs r, outlier detection with IQR, and a chi-square test are all squarely within the SL syllabus and are appropriate for the research question.  The techniques are relevant but not used in an especially sophisticated way, fitting band 3 (ârelevant mathematics at course level usedâ).

Only two representations are offered: a scatterplot and a comprehensive numerical table.  The scatterplot is appropriate and labelled; the table of sums supports the calculation, but no further visuals (box-plots, residual plot, contingency table heat-map, etc.) are provided.  This constitutes âsome appropriate mathematical representations,â not the âeffectiveâ variety implied by band 3.

The student reflects on why the expected positive correlation did not appear and discusses several limitations (year mismatch, sunscreen usage, age distribution).  These comments go beyond a superficial restatement of results, providing âmeaningful reflection,â though not yet âsubstantial critical reflection.â

Creativity is limited to deciding whether to keep an outlier; otherwise the work applies routine statistical tools.  No inventive modelling or alternative data treatments are attempted, indicating only âsome creativity or initiative.â

The exploration follows a recognisable sequenceâintroduction, data collection, mathematics, conclusionâand the argument progresses in a generally sensible way from data to statistical tests to interpretation.  Although transitions are occasionally abrupt and some explanations (e.g., of the outlier rule) feel inserted rather than integrated, the reader can consistently follow the logic.  Minor digressions and a few computational slips do not seriously disrupt the thread, so the work meets the description of being âcoherent and showing clear logical developmentâ rather than the higher standard of âexcellentâ.

There is a basic critique of data quality and methodology (e.g., year discrepancy, lack of age control) and a judgment on statistical significance.  While thoughtful, the analysis remains descriptive; deeper probing of effect size, confidence intervals, or alternative models would be needed for band 3.

The student links the statistical findings to personal behaviour (âbe more cautious under the sunâ) and broader understanding of melanoma risk, showing a clearâthough not extensiveâconnection between mathematics and real-world insight.

A Table of Contents, section headings and numbered subsections provide a clear skeleton.  Each major part (Introduction, Data, Mathematics, Conclusion) is present, and the bibliography is separated.  Nevertheless, there are lapsesâe.g., a blank row in the data table, some duplicated horizontal rules, and the mathematical work is not sub-headedâso the flow is solid rather than âexceptionally well organised.â

Personal motivation (living in the âsunshine stateâ) is described in the introduction, yet the analytic pathway does not reflect an individual strategy; it follows a conventional statistics workflow.  Thus there is âsome evidence of personal approachâ but not a clear, driving personal perspective.

annotations.comment: Presenting the full xy, xÂ², and yÂ² breakdown in a table demonstrates a systematic use of mathematics for Pearsonâs calculation.
The title is concise and directly reflects the research focus, aiding reader orientation at the outset.
Including the word count here disrupts flow; consider omitting or placing administrative details separately.
The research question clearly specifies variables but could mention the year mismatch upfront for precision.
The table of contents lacks page numbers, reducing navigational clarity; adding them would strengthen structure.
Empty cells in the contents table appear; remove or complete them to improve presentation coherence.
The aim statement precisely identifies x and y variables and methods, giving clear direction to the exploration.
Personal anecdote about living in the âsunshine stateâ adds engagement but could be more tightly linked to methodology.
Definitions of external concepts (e.g., scatterplots) in the introduction add bulk; move them to the methods section.
Method plan refers to âdoing the outlier formulaâ but lacks detail on which dataset and order; specify the step-by-step sequence.
Section headings would improve readability within the Mathematics section rather than a single block of text.
The data table spans two pages and even includes a blank row after Oregon; remove empty rows to maintain consistency.
Including all 50 states shows relevant mathematics at course level and completeness of data collection.
The narrative under 'Scatterplot' merely defines it; interpret key features (e.g., point clusters, trend line) to deepen analysis.
Consider adding additional representations (e.g., box plot, residual plot) to examine distribution and deviations more effectively.
Caption citation for the scatterplot is missing full source details; include complete attribution following a chosen style.
The application of the 1.5 IQR rule is appropriate; the formula is clearly stated with correct notation.
The IQR calculation uses Qâ rather than Qâ in the lower bound; ensure Qâ=3.8 is used for the expression Qââ1.5Â·IQR.
Inconsistent Qâ values (5.7 vs earlier 5.8) appear; maintain consistency to avoid confusion in statistical communication.
The table is very dense and overwhelms the reader; consider summarizing key sum totals and moving raw data to an appendix.
Column headers like â<math>x^2</math>â lack subscripts for individual observations (e.g., x_iÂ²); use precise notation to enhance clarity.
Typo in the substituted formula uses â\sum 44â instead of â\sum xâ; correct variable notation for accuracy.
The Pearson result is rounded to three significant figures correctly, demonstrating appropriate mathematical communication.
The statement â0.0 < r < -0.3â is logically incorrect; use â-0.3 < r < 0.0â when describing a weak negative correlation.
Hypotheses are described in prose; define Hâ and Hâ using symbolic notation to enhance mathematical precision.
Degrees of freedom formula is correctly applied, showing understanding of chi-square prerequisites.
The rationale for Yates correction is briefly mentioned; expand on why small sample size warrants this adjustment.
Observed frequencies table is well labeled and supports the chi-square calculation clearly.
Grouping by mean values needs justification; discuss why splitting at 4.88 and 23.9 is statistically appropriate.
Expected frequency calculations are correct, demonstrating thorough understanding of contingency table mechanics.
All |OâE| values are identical (1.88); include commentary on why residuals are uniform across cells.
The computed ÏÂ² calc and p-value interpretation is correct, leading logically to failure to reject Hâ.
P-value source (TI-84 output) is not shown; include a screenshot or summary of calculator steps for reproducibility.
Conclusion neatly summarizes findings and ties back to the research question, showing coherence in development.
Identification of age as a confounding variable demonstrates critical reflection on study limitations.
Suggest designing a follow-up study with age-stratified incidence rates to address the highlighted limitation.
Bibliography entries are numerous but inconsistent in formatting; apply a uniform citation style (e.g., APA) for coherence.
The extensive bibliography indicates thorough research across multiple sources, adding credibility to the exploration.

Sophisticated use of multiple mathematical representations
Consistent and accurate mathematical language and notation throughout

Highly personal approach that enhances understanding
Some relevant mathematics at appropriate level

Mathematics is correct with minor errors
